text: Object detection from remote sensing images is an emerging research field in recent years. However, small objects always reside in the background of huge images and correspond to fewer numbers of pixels, which can lead to noisy representations and decrease the detection accuracy. These causes usually cause damage to the normal detection methods. Therefore, improving the detection performance of small objects has become a urgent and important task. In this paper, a light and robust detection algorithm called LAR-YOLOv8 is proposed to boost the performance for local object detection. Our work is mainly dedicated to explore how to improve the performance in terms of precision, accuracy and speed. For this purpose, three components are introduced into LAR-YOLOv8: (1) To enhance the learning ability of network, a dual-branch attention block together with vision transformer block is designed to boost the representation capability of the feature maps. (2) A brand new attention-guided BiFPN which adopts dynamic sparse attention mechanism in the shallow layers and promotes more powerful top-down paths for feature fusion. (3) An improved evaluation metric namely RIOU (Re-Intersection over Union) loss that is aimed at preventing the instability of gradient and enhances the confidence of bounding box shape for the predictions and the truth samples. Experimental results on three benchmark datasets including NWPU VHR-10, RSOD and CARPK show that LAR-YOLOv8 obtains state-of-the-art performance on all three datasets, especially in terms of mAP (small) and overall mAP as well as with respect to the speed, which verifies the effectiveness of proposed architectures in contrast to the YOLOv8 baseline.
